claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'd like to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Can you tell me how old Alex is?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Alex is 18, I need to understand his current situation to determine eligibility.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training? (For example, is he studying at school, college, or in an approved training program?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He finished school and isn't studying anything right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have a few more questions to determine Alex's eligibility.

Is Alex currently working, and if so, how many hours per week does he work?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't working at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One more important question:

Is Alex receiving any benefits in his own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not receiving any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me also confirm: does Alex live with you in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex lives with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

Here's why: While you normally qualify for Child Benefit for children under 16, the rules are different for older children. For children aged 16 or over, you can still receive Child Benefit if they are under 20 **and** they stay in approved education or training.

Since Alex is 18 but is not currently in any approved education or training, he does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

However, if Alex's circumstances change in the future - for example, if he decides to return to college, sixth form, or starts an approved training program - you may be able to claim Child Benefit again at that point, as long as he remains under 20 years old.

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old. According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for children under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify for Child Benefit if the child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 and is not currently in any approved education or training, he does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. The fact that he is not working and not receiving benefits in his own right does not change this - the key requirement is that children aged 16-19 must be in approved education or training to qualify.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old. Alex lives with the claimant in the UK but is not currently in approved education or training. Under Child Benefit rules, children aged 16 or over only qualify if they are under 20 and remain in approved education or training. Since Alex does not meet this requirement, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for him.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old. According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for children under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify for Child Benefit if the child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 and is not currently in any approved education or training, he does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. The fact that he is not working and not receiving benefits in his own right does not change this - the key requirement is that children aged 16-19 must be in approved education or training to qualify.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old. Alex lives with the claimant in the UK but is not currently in approved education or training. Under Child Benefit rules, children aged 16 or over only qualify if they are under 20 and remain in approved education or training. Since Alex does not meet this requirement, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for him.
